segments.caption: The video opens with a black screen, then transitions into a stylized montage of Cristiano Ronaldo’s life and career. It begins with a young Cristiano in a black-and-white photo, wearing a white and black sports jersey with 'AQUIMAR' printed on it, standing in front of a family portrait and a scenic backdrop of a church on a rocky hill. The scene shifts to a sepia-toned image of a teenage Cristiano in a green-and-white striped jersey, with a younger version of himself and a playing action shot beside him, set against a grand architectural background. The next frame shows a red-tinted image of Cristiano in a Manchester United jersey, celebrating with an open mouth, flanked by two other versions of himself in similar poses. This is followed by a split-screen image: on the left, a young Cristiano in a black-and-white jersey with a whistle in his mouth; on the right, a mature Cristiano in a red jersey holding a large silver trophy. The montage then cuts to real-action footage: a close-up of a young Cristiano in a blue training environment, followed by a dynamic sequence of him playing in a red Manchester United jersey, dribbling, scoring a goal, and celebrating with teammates. The final frames show him in a suit holding the Ballon d'Or trophy, then a title card with the word 'balón' in red, and finally, the text 'CRISTIANO ASCENDING TO GREATNESS' in bold white letters on a black background. The visual style evolves from nostalgic, textured stills to vibrant, high-energy live-action clips, culminating in a dramatic title reveal.

discovered_new_entities.Cristiano Ronaldo: Appears in multiple stages of life: as a young boy in a black-and-white 'AQUIMAR' jersey, as a teenager in a green-and-white striped jersey, as a young adult in a red Manchester United jersey with 'vodafone' and 'AIG' logos, and finally as a mature man in a suit holding the Ballon d'Or trophy. His expressions range from serious to celebratory, and his actions include playing, scoring, and receiving awards.

The narrative then shifts to his early football journey, showing a close-up of his regional football license from 'Clube Futebol Andorinha' with the name 'Cristiano Ronaldo dos Santos Aveiro' and the years '92-95' highlighted. A photo of a teenage Cristiano with his father appears, followed by the logo of Marítimo Madeira, a prominent local club, and then the logo of another club, possibly his next step in development. The visuals are warm, grainy, and evocative of the 1980s–1990s, emphasizing his roots and formative years in Madeira.

segments.caption: The video opens with a title card introducing Aurelio Pereira, Chief Scout of Sporting CP, set against a dark, textured background with the club’s green crest featuring a lion. The narrative quickly shifts to highlight Sporting’s legacy by displaying a collage of iconic players—Futre, Figo, Quaresma, Nani, and Moutinho—each framed in white with their names in bold white text, emphasizing the club’s history of producing world-class talent. The scene transitions to an outdoor setting where a man in a green sweater walks past a large white lion statue, symbolizing Sporting’s emblem, under a clear sky. This is followed by a split-screen graphic showing the logos of two clubs: a dark blue shield with vertical stripes (likely a rival or former club) and Sporting’s green crest, with the transfer fee '€25000' appearing prominently. Two young boys, one in a white-and-black striped jersey and the other in a green-and-white striped jersey, are shown beneath the fee, suggesting a youth transfer. The video then cuts to a man walking across a sunlit football pitch with green stands in the background, followed by a close-up of his feet stepping on the white line. A panoramic view of a cityscape with a river and historic buildings appears, overlaid with a faded image of a youth football match. The final frames show a young player in a green-and-white striped jersey dribbling past an opponent in a red-and-white striped jersey, with 'VULCANO' visible on the background banner, before cutting to the man in the green sweater sitting in a dugout, looking directly at the camera with a serious expression, reinforcing his role as a scout or figure of authority.

segments.caption: The video opens with a black-and-white group photo of a youth football team in green-and-white striped jerseys, with Young Cristiano Ronaldo standing in the back row, looking focused and determined, marking his early integration into the Sporting CP youth academy. The scene transitions to archival footage of young boys in green Reebok hoodies walking together, followed by a close-up of a young Ronaldo in a green training kit, showcasing his early dedication. The narrative then shifts to a split-screen of two young Ronaldo images: one kneeling with a trophy and another holding a stone football trophy, both in Sporting CP jerseys, emphasizing his early achievements. Text overlays reveal 'APRIL 2001' and the Sporting CP crest, followed by the introduction of José Veiga, identified as Ronaldo’s agent, and details of a '4-YEAR DEAL' for '€2,000 PER MONTH', highlighting the modest beginnings of his professional contract. The video then transitions to a close-up of Ronaldo’s face with '2001-2002' overlaid, followed by scenes of him walking with a man in a blue shirt near a stadium, and later training in a blue Reebok jersey. The segment concludes with a formal portrait of László Bölöni, labeled as 'SPORTING MANAGER', underscoring his role in Ronaldo’s early professional development. The setting shifts from nostalgic black-and-white photos to vibrant outdoor training grounds and formal portraits, reflecting the progression from youth to professional football.

segments.caption: The video opens with a formal portrait of László Bölöni, labeled as 'SPORTING MANAGER', standing beside a young Cristiano Ronaldo in a Reebok tracksuit, setting the stage for a critical evaluation of Ronaldo’s early professional development. A handwritten report titled 'Report: Cristiano Ronaldo 2001-2002' appears on lined paper, listing his negatives under Technique, Physical, Tactics, and Mental categories—highlighting issues like poor aerial game, insufficient technique, lack of strength, no tactical awareness, and selfishness. The scene transitions to archival footage of Ronaldo training with teammates in Reebok gear, jogging and running drills on a grass field, with a close-up of Bölöni giving instructions to Ronaldo during a rainy session. The narrative shifts to a celebratory moment with Ronaldo and teammates on a bus labeled 'LISBOA VISION', surrounded by cheering fans waving green-and-white flags, indicating a victory parade. The video then introduces key figures: Nuno Gomes and Rui Costa are shown in a split-screen with the Sporting CP crest, followed by a scene of Ronaldo being physically confronted by a teammate in a locker room, suggesting internal conflict. The segment concludes with a dramatic introduction of Jorge Mendes, shown in a dark suit and sunglasses, with his name displayed prominently against a green grid background, symbolizing his rising influence in Ronaldo’s career.

segments.caption: The video opens with a dark green, grid-patterned background featuring the Sporting CP crest, introducing Jorge Mendes in a suit and sunglasses, labeled 'MENDES', as the central figure. A hierarchical diagram unfolds, connecting Mendes to three young players: Quaresma, Viana, and Cristiano Ronaldo, each shown in youth football attire. The diagram then branches to include José Veiga, another agent, positioned below Mendes. The scene transitions to a vertical column of European football club crests—AC Milan, Juventus, Inter, Real Madrid, Barcelona, Liverpool, Arsenal, and Manchester United—flanked by euro symbols, suggesting transfer negotiations. The focus narrows to Cristiano Ronaldo and Mendes, then shifts to Ronaldo with his mother, followed by José Veiga and his mother, with the text 'SEPTEMBER 2002' appearing. The timeline expands to '2002-2003', and the video introduces Mario Jardel, a player in a Sporting CP jersey, followed by a man in a beige suit pointing at a tactical football formation. The name 'FREITAS' appears above a man in a suit, then 'CRISTIANO' below him, before cutting to archival footage of a young Ronaldo in a yellow-and-black training kit performing a wide leg stretch on a grass field.

Cristiano Ronaldo: The Biography.' The visuals maintain a dark, textured background with grid patterns, emphasizing the high-stakes transfer market and Ronaldo’s rising international profile.

segments.caption: The video segment opens with a close-up of a young Cristiano Ronaldo in a red Portugal national team jersey with the number 7, set against a dark red background featuring the Manchester United crest. A quote appears: 'A DREAM TO BE ABLE TO PLAY FOR A CLUB THAT REPRESENTS SO MUCH TRADITION,' attributed to Cristiano Ronaldo from 'Balague, Guillem. Cristiano Ronaldo: The Biography.' The scene transitions to a dark blue background with a grid pattern, introducing Luis Suárez, labeled as 'INTER LEGEND/INTER MILAN ADVISER,' shown in a suit with a serious expression. His quote, 'WE HAVE TO SIGN HIM,' appears, followed by 'HE’LL BE ONE OF THE BEST PLAYERS IN THE WORLD,' emphasizing his endorsement. The next frame shows a smiling man with glasses, identified as a football figure, alongside a young player in an Inter Milan jersey, suggesting a connection to the club. The scene shifts to another man in a suit with glasses, standing beside a player in a Juventus jersey, with the Juventus logo in the background. The video then presents a split-screen: on the left, a young Ronaldo in a black-and-white striped Parma jersey with '€8.00M' displayed, and on the right, the Real Madrid crest. A man in a suit appears on the right, gesturing thoughtfully. The final frames show a man in a suit with glasses, pointing, with a purple background featuring the FC Barcelona crest. Four framed photos appear sequentially: Quaresma, Tiago, Deco, and Cristiano, highlighting a group of Portuguese players linked to Barcelona, with the focus narrowing to Quaresma and Cristiano. The atmosphere is dramatic and analytical, using dark, textured backgrounds and bold white text to emphasize key quotes and transfer narratives.

The scene shifts to a green background with the Sporting CP crest, showing Dein and Freitas in framed photos, with a jagged white line symbolizing a broken connection. segments.caption: The video opens with an aerial shot of Emirates Stadium, establishing the setting before transitioning to a bold title card: 'THE UNITED STORY SUMMER 2003' in glowing red text on a black background. segments.caption: The video opens with a split-screen graphic featuring the Manchester United and Sporting CP crests, symbolizing a transfer connection, with bidirectional arrows indicating mutual interest. The scene transitions to a close-up of Jim Ryan, Reserve Team Coach at United, standing beside Sir Alex Ferguson, who is gesturing thoughtfully. Text overlays identify Ryan and quote him: 'I’ve seen a player... I think he’s a winger, but he’s playing as a central striker in the youth team,' followed by 'I wouldn’t wait long to declare our interest,' sourced from 'Cristiano Ronaldo: The Biography.' The narrative shifts to Ferguson on a phone call, with a photo of young Cristiano Ronaldo appearing beside him, and an arrow pointing to the Sporting CP crest, indicating United’s pursuit. A montage of competing clubs—Juventus, Real Madrid, Liverpool, Arsenal, Barcelona, and Valencia—appears below, highlighting the transfer race. The scene cuts to a real-life moment of Ferguson and Ronaldo walking near a bridge, then back to a graphic of the two clubs facing off at Estádio José Alvalade on August 6, 2003. The final frames show a split-screen of Ferguson and a man in sunglasses (likely a Sporting CP representative) on August 5, 2003, underscoring the critical timing of the transfer negotiations.

segments.caption: The video opens with a dramatic, stylized graphic featuring Sir Alex Ferguson’s face in a white-bordered frame against a textured red background with a grid overlay, setting a serious, strategic tone. The scene evolves into a split-screen format, with the left side displaying the Sporting Portugal crest and a green background, while the right side remains red. Two framed portraits appear on the left: first, a man in a dark suit (Jorge Mendes), followed by another man in a light shirt (José Veiga). On the right, Ferguson’s portrait reappears, now under the Manchester United crest, followed by a young Cristiano Ronaldo in a green-and-white Sporting CP jersey. A third figure, a man in a dark suit with sunglasses holding a phone (Man in Sunglasses - Sporting CP Representative), appears on the far right, symbolizing the negotiation side. The graphic transitions to real-world footage: Ferguson smiling on a sunny training ground, then being interviewed by reporters on the pitch, wearing a grey Manchester United training shirt with 'vodafone' sponsorship. The scene cuts to Ronaldo in action—celebrating a goal in a black-and-white Sporting CP kit, then playing in a green-and-white striped jersey, dribbling past opponents and scoring, with the ball hitting the net. The footage is grainy, capturing the intensity of early professional matches, with visible sponsor banners like 'Coca-Cola' and 'Varilux' in the background.

segments.caption: The video opens with archival match footage from a Manchester United vs. Arsenal game on September 21, 2003, showing Cristiano Ronaldo in a red Manchester United jersey (number 7) dribbling past an Arsenal defender in a yellow kit. The scene transitions to a split-screen image of Roy Keane hugging a young Ronaldo in a training top, juxtaposed with Sir Alex Ferguson embracing Ronaldo in a similar moment, highlighting mentorship and integration into the team. A close-up follows of Ronaldo in a white training top with 'RONALDO' on the back, being embraced by Ferguson, emphasizing emotional connection. The narrative shifts to a quote from Roy Keane: 'He was immediately one of the hardest working players at United,' displayed over a red grid background with Keane’s image. The video then cuts to Ronaldo scoring a goal in a match against Portsmouth, with on-screen text indicating 'UNITED 3-0 P'MOUTH, RONALDO - 80’'. A graphic appears showing Ronaldo’s stats: 'UNTIL JANUARY, 19 CAPS, 11 STARTS'. Ferguson is shown clapping, then a graphic displays the EPL 03/04 standings with United in third place (75 points), behind Arsenal (90) and Chelsea (79). The segment ends with a quick shot of Ronaldo scoring a goal in a match against Chelsea, reinforcing his growing impact.

segments.caption: The video opens with a graphic of the EPL 03/04 standings, highlighting Manchester United in third place with 75 points, behind Arsenal and Chelsea. The scene transitions to a warm embrace between Sir Alex Ferguson and a young Cristiano Ronaldo, who is wearing a Manchester United jersey with 'RONALDO 7' on the back, symbolizing their mentor-mentee relationship. A tactical formation graphic follows, showing United's 4-4-2 setup with Ronaldo on the right wing, alongside players like Van Nistelrooy, Scholes, and Keane. The narrative shifts to live match footage from the 2004 FA Cup Final, where Ronaldo, in a red United jersey, scores a goal against Millwall in the 44th minute, followed by a celebration with Ferguson and the team lifting the FA Cup trophy. The segment then transitions to a black screen with glowing text 'EURO SUMMER 2004', introducing Portugal's Euro 2004 campaign. A portrait of Luiz Scolari appears, followed by the Portugal squad list, with Ronaldo listed as number 17. The video cuts to Ronaldo training with the national team, laughing and interacting with teammates, before displaying a quote from Scolari: 'He’s a good kid, but someone must’ve told him he’s the best in the world,' underscoring the early perception of Ronaldo’s confidence and talent.

segments.caption: The video opens with a quote from Luiz Scolari, displayed in bold white text against a dark red grid background, stating, 'AND IF HE THINKS HE IS, IT’LL BE VERY HARD TO WORK WITH HIM,' with Scolari’s portrait on the left. The scene transitions to a montage of Cristiano Ronaldo in his Portugal national team jersey (number 17, red with green trim, Nike logo, and a yellow armband), set against the same red grid backdrop. Flags of Greece, Russia, and Spain appear sequentially, followed by match results: Portugal 1-2 Greece, Russia 0-2 Portugal, and Spain 0-1 Portugal, each accompanied by brief clips of match action and Ronaldo’s reactions—celebrating, frustrated, or intense. The sequence continues with Portugal 2-1 England (penalty shootout 6-5) and Portugal 2-1 Ukraine, with Ronaldo shown shirtless in celebration. A black-and-white team photo of the Portugal squad is shown, with Ronaldo in the front row, followed by a clip of a 0-1 loss to Greece, where Ronaldo is seen dejected. The segment ends with Ronaldo screaming in frustration, emphasizing the emotional highs and lows of his Euro 2004 campaign.

segments.caption: The video segment opens with a dramatic black-and-white image of Cristiano Ronaldo in a Portugal jersey, screaming in frustration during a match, with a score overlay showing '0-1' and the Greek flag, hinting at a loss in Euro 2004. The scene transitions to a close-up of a younger Ronaldo in a Manchester United training top (number 10), smiling confidently, establishing his early days at the club. The narrative then shifts to a quote from Mike Phelan, displayed over a dark red textured background, emphasizing Ronaldo’s physical transformation: 'His body shape was different, his facial features were different' and 'That’s an athletic physique for the British game.' This is followed by footage of Ronaldo in a red Manchester United jersey with 'vodafone' sponsorship, running during training, showcasing his athleticism. The segment then cuts to a quote from Gary Neville, questioning Ronaldo’s defensive commitment: 'Do you live with him for another 12 months? Where he’s doing OK but not scoring 25 goals? Where he can turn off and not defend?' This is intercut with archival match footage from 2004-2005, including games against Fulham, Blackburn, and Bolton, where Ronaldo is seen dribbling, shooting, and scoring, with on-screen graphics indicating the match dates and scores. The footage highlights his dynamic play and goal-scoring ability, reinforcing the narrative of his rising talent and the growing scrutiny around his defensive responsibilities.

segments.caption: The video segment opens with intense action from the 2006 World Cup quarterfinals between England and Portugal on July 1, 2006. Wayne Rooney, in a white England jersey, is shown committing a high tackle on a Portuguese player, resulting in a red card at the 62nd minute. The referee, in a yellow shirt, shows the red card as Cristiano Ronaldo, in a maroon Portugal jersey with number 17, reacts with visible frustration and gestures toward the referee. The scene shifts to Ronaldo’s perspective as he looks on, then cuts to a penalty kick where Portugal’s goalkeeper dives to his right, but the ball goes into the net, giving Portugal a 1-0 lead. The scoreboard updates to 'ENG 0(1-3)0 POR' at full time, indicating a 3-1 penalty shootout win for Portugal. The crowd erupts in celebration, with Portuguese fans cheering wildly. The camera then focuses on a dejected England manager, Sven-Göran Eriksson, walking off the pitch with a hand to his face. The segment transitions to a graphic with Ronaldo in a dark training shirt, hand on his chin, with a quote: 'They told me to push further forward to make the most of the numerical advantage and that’s how I responded.' This is followed by a newspaper-style headline: 'UGLY FACE OF SPORT' and 'PRESSURE BUILDS ON RONALDO,' with a subheading 'Friends, fall-outs, and fearsome feuds,' suggesting controversy surrounding Ronaldo. The final frame shows Ronaldo standing with hands on hips, looking serious, as England’s Wayne Rooney walks past with a medical bag, highlighting the aftermath of the red card incident.

The next shot shows the two standing together in the rain, Ronaldo now in a black training top, both looking focused. The video then cuts to a graphic featuring four legendary Manchester United players—Law, Best, RVN (Ruud van Nistelrooy), and Ole (Ole Gunnar Solskjær)—in vintage red kits, emphasizing the club’s storied history. This is followed by archival match footage of a player in a red kit scoring a goal against a blue team, with the ball entering the net. The sequence returns to Ronaldo in his red AIG jersey, with tactical diagrams appearing beside him, illustrating attacking movements and defensive positioning. The final frames show Ronaldo celebrating with a thumbs-up, his jersey displaying 'RONALDO 7', and on-screen text indicating '06/07' and '23', likely referencing his goal tally for the 2006-2007 season.

segments.caption: The video opens with a stylized graphic of Cristiano Ronaldo in a red Manchester United jersey with 'RONALDO 7' on the back, giving a thumbs-up against a dark red textured background, with on-screen text displaying 'AUG 07-JAN 08' and '06/07' alongside the numbers '24' and '23', likely indicating goal tallies. The scene transitions to live match footage from a Premier League game, where Ronaldo, in a red AIG-sponsored jersey, scores a goal against Wigan Athletic, with the on-screen text '07/08 PREMIER LEAGUE - GW9 UNITED 4-0 WIGAN OCT 6, 2007' confirming the match. He celebrates with teammates, including Saha, who is seen in a red jersey with 'SAHA 9' on the back. The video then cuts to a Champions League match against Dynamo Kiev, where Ronaldo scores again, with the text '07/08 CHAMPIONS LEAGUE - GROUP F D. KIEV 2-4 UNITED OCT 23, 2007' displayed. He is shown celebrating with arms raised. Next, footage from a Premier League match against Blackburn Rovers shows Ronaldo scoring a goal, with the text '07/08 PREMIER LEAGUE - GW13 UNITED 2-0 B'BURN NOV 11, 2007' appearing. He celebrates with arms outstretched. The sequence continues with a match against Fulham, where Ronaldo scores twice, with on-screen text '07/08 PREMIER LEAGUE - GW15 UNITED 1-0 FULHAM RONALDO - 10'' and 'UNITED 2-0 FULHAM RONALDO - 58'' marking the goals. He celebrates with teammates, including Giggs, who is seen in a red jersey with 'GIGGS 11' on the back. The final clip shows Ronaldo scoring a goal in a match against Chelsea, with the text 'UNITED - CHELSEA' displayed, followed by a close-up of him celebrating with arms raised. The stadium is packed with fans, and advertising boards for Heineken, PlayStation, MasterCard, Ford, and Sony Bravia are visible throughout the matches, indicating a professional, high-stakes environment. The lighting is bright and consistent with daytime or well-lit evening matches, and the atmosphere is electric with crowd noise and celebration.

segments.caption: The video opens with a dynamic action shot of Cristiano Ronaldo in a red Manchester United jersey with 'AIG' sponsorship, scoring a goal against Everton on December 23, 2007, as indicated by the on-screen text '07/08 PREMIER LEAGUE - GW18 UNITED 2-1 EVERTON'. He celebrates with a powerful, clenched-fist gesture, his face contorted in triumph, as the crowd roars in the background. The scene transitions to a stylized graphic of the 2007 Ballon d'Or results, showing Ronaldo in a black leather jacket in first place with 444 votes, flanked by Kaka (2nd, 277 votes) and Lionel Messi (3rd, 255 votes), all set against a red grid backdrop. The narrative then shifts to a Champions League match against Lyon on March 4, 2008, where Ronaldo, in a white away kit, scores a goal, followed by a celebration with his teammates. The next sequence shows a Champions League quarterfinal against Roma on April 1, 2008, with Ronaldo scoring in the 39th minute, as the on-screen text confirms 'ROMA 0-1 UNITED RONALDO - 39''. The goal is followed by a wide-angle replay of the play and a close-up of Wayne Rooney waving to the crowd in celebration. The video then presents a split-screen graphic contrasting Manchester United and FC Barcelona, with Ronaldo in a red jersey on the left and Messi in a blue-and-red Barcelona jersey on the right, each with their respective club crests. This is followed by a tactical formation graphic showing the starting lineups for both teams, with Ronaldo and Tevez leading United's attack, and Messi and Eto'o for Barcelona, set against a red and purple grid background. The segment concludes with a close-up of Park Ji-sung in a red Manchester United jersey, looking focused, as the tactical diagram remains on screen.

The narrative then shifts to the second leg at Old Trafford on April 29, 2008, where Paul Scholes scores in the 14th minute, giving United a 1-0 lead, and the crowd erupts in celebration. The video then cuts to the 2007/08 Premier League match against Wigan on May 11, 2008, where Ronaldo, now in a red home kit, scores a penalty in the 32nd minute, and later celebrates with teammates after a 2-0 victory. The final scene shows Ronaldo and teammates joyfully celebrating with fans, wearing red jerseys and scarves, as confetti falls, marking the culmination of a successful season.

segments.caption: The video opens with a celebratory scene of Manchester United players, including Cristiano Ronaldo, lifting the Premier League trophy adorned with a 'Barclays' ribbon, amidst a jubilant crowd. The scene transitions to the 2008 Champions League Final between Manchester United and Chelsea, with on-screen text confirming the match details: '2008 CHAMPIONS LEAGUE FINAL UNITED - CHELSEA MAY 21, 2008'. Tactical formations for both teams are displayed: Manchester United's 4-4-2 with Ronaldo, Tevez, and Carrick highlighted, and Chelsea's 4-4-2 with Drogba, Lampard, and Terry. The action shifts to the pitch, where Ronaldo, in his red Manchester United jersey with 'AIG' sponsorship, skillfully dribbles past a Chelsea defender in a blue 'SAMSUNG' jersey. He then scores a goal with a powerful shot, and the on-screen text confirms 'UNITED 1-0 CHELSEA RONALDO - 26''. The match continues with Chelsea's Frank Lampard equalizing in the 45th minute, as shown by the text 'UNITED 1-1 CHELSEA LAMPARD - 45''. The video then shows Ronaldo attempting a shot that is saved by Chelsea's goalkeeper, Petr Cech, followed by a close-up of Ronaldo looking dejected. The final frames depict Ronaldo in the rain, visibly frustrated, as the match progresses under heavy rainfall, with fans holding umbrellas in the background.

segments.caption: The video opens with a dramatic moment from the 2008 Champions League Final, showing Cristiano Ronaldo in a red Manchester United jersey, visibly distraught after a missed opportunity, with rain soaking his jersey and the net in the foreground. The scene quickly shifts to a close-up of a Chelsea player in a blue jersey, looking dejected, followed by a goalkeeper in green raising his arm in frustration. The narrative then transitions to a celebratory montage: Ronaldo, still in his red Manchester United jersey, is shown lifting the Champions League trophy amidst a crowd of jubilant teammates and fans, with confetti falling and rain continuing to fall. He is seen embracing Sir Alex Ferguson, both wearing medals, and later celebrating with a wide smile, holding the trophy close. The video then cuts to a graphic displaying Ronaldo’s 2007-2008 season stats: 34 Premier League appearances with 31 goals and 9 assists, 11 Champions League appearances with 8 goals and 1 assist, and 3 FA Cup appearances with 3 goals. This is followed by a dynamic shot of Ronaldo celebrating a goal with a clenched fist, his jersey bearing the 'AIG' sponsorship. The segment concludes with a title card reading 'EUROS AND THE 6TH SEASON 2007-2008' in glowing red text, followed by a quote from Carlos Queiroz: 'IF YOU GET FIVE YEARS OUT OF HIM, YOU’VE STRUCK GOLD,' and then 'THERE’S NO PRECEDENT FOR IT,' both displayed over a dark red grid background with Queiroz’s image. The final frames show Ronaldo smiling beside Ferguson, both wearing medals, and a quote from Ronaldo himself: 'I HAVE SAID MILLIONS OF TIMES THAT I WOULD LOVE TO PLAY IN SPAIN,' displayed over a red grid background with his image.

segments.caption: The video opens with a celebratory scene of Cristiano Ronaldo, wearing a red Manchester United jersey with 'AIG' sponsorship and the number 7, joyfully spraying champagne from a bottle as he celebrates with teammates, including Ferdinand, on a green pitch under bright stadium lights. The scene transitions to Ronaldo smiling beside Sir Alex Ferguson, who is wearing a dark polo shirt with the Portuguese national team crest, both appearing happy and soaked in rain. This is followed by a group shot of the Portuguese national team, including Ronaldo, in red jerseys with green trim, standing together and smiling. The narrative then shifts to a graphic of the UEFA Euro 2008 Group A standings, showing Portugal and Turkey tied at 6 points, with Czech Republic and Switzerland at 3 points each. A match-up graphic appears, pitting Portugal against Germany, with Ronaldo and a German player shown in their respective national team kits. The video then cuts to live match footage of a German player scoring, followed by Ronaldo reacting with frustration on the pitch. The final score 'POR 2-3 GER' is displayed, indicating Portugal's loss in the quarterfinals. The segment concludes with a series of split-screen graphics featuring portraits of Sir Alex Ferguson, Cristiano Ronaldo, and other figures, including a man in sunglasses, set against a red grid background with a map of Germany, followed by a close-up of Ferguson in a white shirt with the Manchester United crest, and finally a quote from Ferguson: 'IF SOMEONE COMES, AND OFFERS A WORLD RECORD FEE, THEN DON'T MESS US ABOUT,' sourced from his autobiography.

segments.caption: The video opens with a quote from Sir Alex Ferguson, displayed in bold white text against a dark red grid background, stating, 'AND SOMEONE COMES AND OFFERS A WORLD RECORD FEE, THEN WE WILL LET YOU GO,' with Ferguson’s portrait on the left, wearing a white shirt and red-striped tie, his hand to his face in a thoughtful pose. The scene transitions to a smiling Cristiano Ronaldo and Ferguson seated together at an event, both in formal suits, Ronaldo in a black suit with a pink tie, Ferguson in a red-striped tie, with a lanyard around his neck. The focus then shifts to Ronaldo in a red Manchester United jersey with 'AIG' sponsorship, first shown in a close-up, then in a split-screen image celebrating with himself, followed by a graphic overlay of 'MARCH 2008' as he is shown in action on the pitch. The narrative continues with Ronaldo lying on the grass in pain after a tackle, then walking with crutches in a white t-shirt and black shorts, visibly injured. He is later shown in a black Manchester United training jersey with the number 10, looking intense. The video cuts to a close-up of Ronaldo in a white shirt, then to match footage of him scoring a goal, followed by a celebration with teammates. A quote from Gary Neville appears: 'HE WAS VERY PROFESSIONAL THAT WHOLE SEASON,' then 'BUT HE WAS COUNTING DOWN THE MONTHS UNTIL HE COULD LEAVE,' both overlaid on Neville’s portrait in a red jersey. The segment ends with Ronaldo in a red jersey, looking frustrated after a match, with rain visibly soaking his jersey, and a final close-up of his determined expression.

segments.caption: The video opens with a dynamic football match scene, showing Cristiano Ronaldo in a red Manchester United jersey with 'AIG' sponsorship, celebrating a goal with a wide smile and raised arms. The scene transitions to a close-up of Ronaldo’s face behind a goal net, with on-screen text indicating '08/09 PREMIER LEAGUE - GW13 UNITED 5-0 STOKE' and 'CRISTIANO'S 100TH UNITED GOAL'. The narrative then shifts to a formal setting where Ronaldo, now in a dark suit and tie, holds the Ballon d'Or trophy, kissing it affectionately in front of a 'FRANCE football' backdrop. A graphic follows, showing Ronaldo flanked by two other players, with text indicating '1ST 446', '2ND 281', and '3RD 129' for the '2008 BALLON D'OR'. Subsequent frames feature Ronaldo with quotes: 'THIS TROPHY IS SO IMPORTANT THAT I WANT TO WIN IT AGAIN' and 'I’LL WAKE UP EVERY DAY AND SAY TO MYSELF, “I HAVE TO KEEP IMPROVING.”' The video then cuts to a match scene with Ronaldo in a red jersey, followed by a goal celebration in the 2008 FIFA Club World Cup semifinals against Osaka, with text 'OSAKA 3-5 UNITED DEC 18, 2008'. The final scenes show Ronaldo scoring in the 2009 Champions League Round of 16 against Inter, with text 'UNITED 2 (2-0) 0 INTER MAR 11, 2009', and then a goal against Porto in the quarterfinals, with text 'PORTO 0 (2-3) 1 UNITED APR 15, 2009'. The environment shifts from a vibrant stadium to a formal award ceremony and back to intense match action, with lighting varying from bright stadium lights to the softer glow of the award stage. The atmosphere is celebratory and triumphant, emphasizing Ronaldo’s achievements and relentless drive for improvement.

segments.caption: The video segment opens with a dramatic goal by Cristiano Ronaldo in a 2009 Champions League semifinal against Arsenal, where he scores past the goalkeeper, leading to a 3-1 aggregate victory for Manchester United. The scene transitions to Ronaldo celebrating with teammates and fans, including a joyful moment with his family, holding the Barclays Premier League trophy. The narrative then shifts to the 2009 Champions League Final against Barcelona, where Ronaldo is shown in action, but the match ends in a 2-0 defeat for United, with goals from Eto’o and Messi. The video captures Ronaldo’s emotional reaction to the loss, including a close-up of him covering his face in disappointment. The final frames show the referee signaling the end of the match, followed by a shot of the Barcelona players celebrating their victory, with Messi prominently featured. The atmosphere is tense and emotional, reflecting the high stakes of the final and the contrasting outcomes for the two teams.

segments.caption: The video opens with a wide shot of a football stadium at night, where Cristiano Ronaldo, wearing a white Manchester United away jersey with 'AIG' sponsorship and red boots, stands on the pitch with hands on hips, looking pensive. The camera zooms in for a close-up of his face, capturing a serious, focused expression. The scene cuts to a black screen, then transitions to a graphic split-screen showing Ronaldo on the left and Florentino Pérez (labeled 'CALDERON') on the right, with the Manchester United and Real Madrid crests above them and the transfer fee '€94.00M' displayed prominently. The video then shifts to a daytime street scene with a crowd of fans gathered outside a stadium, followed by a shot of a white car driving through the crowd. A close-up of a man in a suit and glasses appears, then cuts to a young Ronaldo in a black-and-white striped youth jersey playing football on a dirt pitch. The scene transitions to a stadium filled with fans, then to a close-up of Ronaldo in a red Manchester United jersey celebrating a goal with his arms raised. The final shot shows Ronaldo in a white jersey with the number 9 on his back, running down a tunnel toward the pitch, with bright stadium lights ahead.

segments.caption: The video segment begins with a bright white screen that gradually transitions into a credits sequence. The credits are displayed on a light gray grid background, listing the creator, Fahad Al-Thekair, as the writer, director, and narrator. The sources are detailed, including reputable outlets like English Premier League, Sky Sports, The Guardian, Reuters, Getty Images, UEFA, The Athletic, Manchester United Official Website, and Ferguson’s autobiography. The music credits follow, featuring artists such as Yoko Kanno, Madison York, Lejsovka & Freund, Kit Grill, V.V. Campos, Marten Moses, Farrell Wooten, Pye Corner Audio, Richard Pike, Bendik Giske, Tomaga, and Anenon. After the music credits, a 'THANKS TO ALL THE PATRONS' section appears, listing numerous supporters in two columns, including names like Meshal Almutairi, Bobby Donev, and others, with some names in Arabic script. The credits fade to black, and the word 'balón' appears in a stylized, light blue font on a black background, with the 'o' designed as a double ring, suggesting a football. The logo remains for a few seconds before fading out, concluding the segment with a black screen.
